findings.title: Exposed-agent name + template label enumerable by all non-connector MCP keys
findings.description: Dedicated chat_with_<slug> tools register with canAccess=connectorDenied (scope!=='connector'), which admits all user/agent/system-scoped keys. FastMCP filters each session's tool set by canAccess, so any authenticated non-connector key enumerates every exposed agent's tool name + description (name + trinity.template label) regardless of per-agent access. Execution is still gated by checkAgentAccess (no bypass); the leak is metadata/enumeration only.
findings.exploit: Tenant B with an ordinary MCP key issues tools/list and receives chat_with_<other-tenant-agent> entries describing 'Template: github:Org/private-repo' for agents it cannot access, regressing the enumeration-resistance that list_agents enforces.
findings.impact: Cross-tenant disclosure of exposed agent names and template/repo identifiers to unauthorized authenticated users.
findings.recommendation: Scope dedicated-tool canAccess to actual agent access (mirror list_agents), OR drop the template label from build_tool_description, OR document global-visibility semantics in the owner UI.

findings.title: Agent trinity.template label flows unsanitized into advertised tool description
findings.description: build_tool_description embeds the trinity.template label verbatim into the MCP tool description advertised to all connected LLM clients. If freeform text can land in the template label, it becomes a second-order prompt-injection surface. Not promoted: typical template labels are constrained; clean injection path not demonstrated.
findings.recommendation: Confirm the full set of values that can reach trinity.template; folds into Finding 1 rec #2 (name-only description).

clean: Internal poll endpoint gated by router-level verify_internal_secret (constant-time)
Execution access gate (checkAgentAccess) preserved with server-bound agent name
No SQL/command injection (computed slug, SQLAlchemy, static DDL)
Authz separation: GET=read, PUT=owner-only + system-agent refusal + audit
